questions.question: Given a sample of size n from a population with a known standard deviation σ, calculate the sample mean. State the formula and explain its significance.
questions.hint: The Central Limit Theorem states that the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normally distributed with mean μ and standard deviation σ/√n.

questions.question: A survey of 100 students has a sample mean of 75 and a sample standard deviation of 10. Assuming a normal population distribution, calculate the 95% confidence interval for the population mean. State the margin of error and explain its significance.
questions.hint: Use the formula: CI = (x̄ - z * σ/√n, x̄ + z * σ/√n) where z is the z-score corresponding to the desired confidence level.

questions.question: A company claims that the average height of its employees is 175 cm with a standard deviation of 5 cm. Using a sample of 50 employees, calculate the confidence interval for the population mean height at a 99% confidence level. State the margin of error and explain its significance.
questions.hint: Calculate the standard error first: σ/√n = 5/√50 = 0.83. Then, find the z-score corresponding to the desired confidence level (α = 0.01). Use the formula: CI = (x̄ - z * σ/√n, x̄ + z * σ/√n)
